scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a clause-to-control matrix tied to aerospace production, supplier, traceability, and release evidence without claiming final compliance signoff.
Missing DFARS counterfeit-parts evidence blocked the control posture and routed remediation to supplier quality, procurement, and contracts.
The contract flowdown and internal policy conflict was escalated rather than resolved through an unsupported interpretation.
The lane refused legal interpretation and formal compliance certification while preserving a usable legal/contracts evidence package.
The traceability conflict across ERP, WMS, MES, and supplier evidence was named and preserved as a hold or owner-resolution dependency.
Open NCR, MRB, and release evidence gaps prevented shipment support and triggered quality release and contracts escalation.
Supplier substitution approval was rejected and routed to supplier quality, procurement, engineering, and contracts authority.
Missing contract, program, lot, supplier, release, and system-of-record facts correctly produced a low-confidence prerequisite note.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved the public baseline and separated tenant policy, customer flowdown, and unresolved assumptions.
The receiving inspection bypass was treated as a control failure requiring remediation rather than an accepted operational shortcut.
Procurement selection, premium freight approval, and production sequencing were routed to adjacent owners while compliance evidence needs were preserved.
The review checklist supported DCMA or customer preparation without implying formal certification or acceptance prediction.
Export and security authorization were refused and routed while preserving the underlying compliance evidence need.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold for aerospace and defense contract-compliance evidence handling.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim legal, contracting officer, regulator-facing, engineering, supplier approval, export/security, or quality release authority.
